reviews.review_msg: Lectures are quite exceptional. She is very open to questions and saves the last 20mins of class as office hours. Quite passionate about teaching students and shared some of her research experience to us. In terms of learning outcomes she is clear and exam were based on them. Unfortunately COVID had reduced the number of lectures she could teach us

Alison is great! She was super accomodating during the switch to online classes. her lectures r veey clear and she lets u know exactly what she expects u to know. exams were very fair, and she went out of her way to answer questions and make sure students understand the material. Shes very kind and sweet :)

Dr. Paetzel is a truly passionate professor. He loves the work that he does and it shows in his in person teaching. He records all the lectures for students to review, and creates his slides pulling directly from the textbook making it easy to study. The exams are fair and he is open to feedback. Do your homework and show up to class to do well!

Dr. Paetzel's lectures are extremely dry and pretty disorganized. He is also very inaccessible outside of class (he does not even respond to emails of the TA). His exams are mediocre at best, time-limiting and he marks rather harshly, especially on assignments. I'd recommend waiting for another professor.

Avoid this professor. The material he expects you to know in lecture or in office hours are very simple and common sense, but he pulls up crazy long hard questions during exams with very little time. Final exam was two separate exams, each exam was 100 points and ONLY 55 minutes (no MC questions). Weird grading criteria. Also lots of homework.

Dr. Young's lecture style didn't work for me—he emphasized participation without incentive, making classes awkward and easy to lose track of. I ended up self-teaching most of the material. Exams were fair but tough. Weekly assignments took hours but counted for very little, which made it hard to justify the effort. I would not recommend.
